Operation 'createNotification' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is too generic and doesn't convey the multi-channel capability. Description lacks critical details about authentication requirements, expected inputs (recipient types, channel selection), outputs (notification object structure), and side effects (message queuing/delivery). Enhanced version clarifies what channels are supported, authentication needs, input requirements, return value, and delivery behavior to help AI agents determine when and how to use this operation appropriately.

data.current_description: Send push notifications, emails, or SMS messages to your users
data.summary_suggestion: Send notifications to users via multiple channels
data.description_suggestion: Creates and sends notifications to specified users or segments via one or more channels (push notifications, email, SMS, or in-app messaging). Requires API key authentication and valid recipient identifiers (user IDs or segment IDs). Accepts channel-specific content and delivery parameters. Returns a notification object with ID and delivery status. Notifications are queued for immediate or scheduled delivery based on parameters provided.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic and doesn't convey the multi-channel capability. Description lacks critical details about authentication requirements, expected inputs (recipient types, channel selection), outputs (notification object structure), and side effects (message queuing/delivery). Enhanced version clarifies what channels are supported, authentication needs, input requirements, return value, and delivery behavior to help AI agents determine when and how to use this operation appropriately.
